Excellent choice! 🚀

An AI Placement Assistant is exactly the kind of project that can make your resume stand out for companies like Capgemini, TCS, Infosys, Cognizant, Accenture, and Wipro. It combines AI, web development, APIs, and databases—all skills recruiters like to see.

📁 Project Name

AI Placement Assistant

🎯 Objective

A web application that helps students prepare for placements by generating:

  • ATS-friendly resumes

  • Cover letters

  • Technical interview questions

  • HR interview questions

  • MCQ quizzes

  • ATS score estimation

AI ModelGroq API (Llama 3)

📌 Module 1: Resume Generator

  • Name

Example:

Examples:

  • Why should we hire you?

  • Why Capgemini?

  • Describe a challenge you faced.

📌 Module 5: MCQ Quiz Generator

Choose topic:

  • Python

AI generates:

  • Correct Answers

Checks:

  • Keywords

  • Skills

  • Section headings

  • Contact details

Returns:

ATS SCORE : 88%

✓ Contact Information
✓ Professional Summary
✓ Skills
✓ Projects

💼 Resume Description

You can eventually add this to your resume:

AI Placement Assistant

Developed a full-stack AI-powered placement preparation platform using Python, Flask, MySQL, and the Groq API. The application generates ATS-friendly resumes, personalized cover letters, technical interview questions, HR interview preparation material, and basic ATS score analysis through an interactive web interface.

Technologies: Python, Flask, HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, MySQL, Groq API.

Development Plan

Phase 1 (Day 1)

✅ Project Setup

✅ Flask Application

✅ Homepage UI

Phase 2 (Day 2)

✅ Resume Generator

Phase 3 (Day 3)

✅ Cover Letter Generator

Phase 4 (Day 4)

✅ Interview Question Generator

Phase 5 (Day 5)

✅ ATS Score Checker

Phase 6

✅ GitHub Upload

✅ Add screenshots to README

I suggest we build it module by module, starting with the homepage and project structure, then integrating the Groq API. This approach will make the project easier to understand and give you a professional GitHub repository that you can confidently discuss in interviews. 🚀

⭐ Final Resume Description

AI Placement Assistant

Developed a full-stack AI-powered placement preparation platform using Python, Flask, MySQL, and the Groq API. The application provides ATS-friendly resume generation, personalized cover letters, interview question generation, MCQ quizzes, ATS score analysis, skill-gap recommendations, resume parsing, portfolio generation, and company-specific placement preparation through an interactive web interface. Implemented user authentication, history management, PDF export, and responsive dashboard features.

Technologies: Python, Flask, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Bootstrap, MySQL, Groq API, Git, GitHub, Chart.js.

If you complete even 80% of these features, this will be a portfolio-quality project that is strong enough to discuss confidently in interviews and showcase on your GitHub profile for companies like Capgemini and other service-based MNCs. 🚀

Phase 1 : Project Setup

Step 1: Create Folder Structure

Create this manually:

AI-Placement-Assistant/
│
├── app.py
├── config.py
├── requirements.txt
├── database.sql
├── README.md
│
├── templates/
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── dashboard.html
│   ├── login.html
│   ├── register.html
│   ├── resume.html
│   ├── coverletter.html
│   ├── interview.html
│   ├── ats.html
│   └── quiz.html
│
├── static/
│   ├── css/
│   │    └── style.css
│   │
│   ├── js/
│   │    └── script.js
│   │
│   └── images/
│        └── logo.png
│
└── modules/
    ├── resume_generator.py
    ├── coverletter_generator.py
    ├── interview_generator.py
    ├── ats_checker.py
    └── quiz_generator.py

Step 2 : Install Packages

Open CMD:

Bash
mkdir AI-Placement-Assistant

cd AI-Placement-Assistant

python -m venv venv

venv\Scripts\activate

Now install:

Bash
pip install flask
pip install groq
pip install pymysql
pip install python-dotenv
pip install reportlab
pip install flask-login
pip install werkzeug

requirements.txt

Create:

Flask==3.1.0
groq
pymysql
python-dotenv
reportlab
Flask-Login
Werkzeug

Then later:

Bash
pip install -r requirements.txt

Step 3 : config.py

Python
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

class Config:
    SECRET_KEY = "AIPlacementSecret123"

    MYSQL_HOST = "localhost"
    MYSQL_USER = "root"
    MYSQL_PASSWORD = ""
    MYSQL_DB = "placement_ai"

    GROQ_API_KEY = os.getenv("GROQ_API_KEY")

Step 4 : Create .env

In project root:

GROQ_API_KEY=Paste_Your_Groq_API_Key_Here

Never upload this file to GitHub.

Step 5 : app.py

Python
from flask import Flask, render_template
from config import Config

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config.from_object(Config)


@app.route("/")
def home():
    return render_template("index.html")


@app.route("/dashboard")
def dashboard():
    return render_template("dashboard.html")


@app.route("/resume")
def resume():
    return render_template("resume.html")


@app.route("/coverletter")
def coverletter():
    return render_template("coverletter.html")


@app.route("/interview")
def interview():
    return render_template("interview.html")


@app.route("/ats")
def ats():
    return render_template("ats.html")


@app.route("/quiz")
def quiz():
    return render_template("quiz.html")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

Step 6 : templates/index.html

HTML
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>

    <title>AI Placement Assistant</title>

    <link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.3/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css"
        rel="stylesheet">

    <link rel="stylesheet"
        href="{{ url_for('static', filename='css/style.css') }}">

</head>

<body>

<div class="container">

    <h1 class="text-center mt-5">
        AI Placement Assistant
    </h1>

    <p class="text-center">
        Your Personal AI Career Companion
    </p>

    <div class="row mt-5">

        <div class="col-md-4">
            <a href="/resume" class="btn btn-primary w-100 p-3">
                Resume Generator
            </a>
        </div>

        <div class="col-md-4">
            <a href="/coverletter" class="btn btn-success w-100 p-3">
                Cover Letter
            </a>
        </div>

        <div class="col-md-4">
            <a href="/interview" class="btn btn-warning w-100 p-3">
                Interview Questions
            </a>
        </div>

    </div>

    <div class="row mt-4">

        <div class="col-md-6">
            <a href="/ats" class="btn btn-danger w-100 p-3">
                ATS Checker
            </a>
        </div>

        <div class="col-md-6">
            <a href="/quiz" class="btn btn-info w-100 p-3">
                AI Quiz
            </a>
        </div>

    </div>

</div>

</body>

</html>

Step 9 : Run Project

Bash
python app.py

Visit:

http://127.0.0.1:5000

GitHub Repository Name

AI-Placement-Assistant

README Title:

Markdown
# AI Placement Assistant

An AI-powered placement preparation platform built using Flask and Groq API.

## Features

- ATS Resume Generator
- AI Cover Letter Generator
- Technical Interview Generator
- ATS Score Checker
- AI Quiz Generator

## Technologies

Python, Flask, HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, MySQL, Groq API

Our Development Roadmap

Phase 1 ✅

  • Project Setup

  • Homepage UI

  • Navigation

Phase 2

  • User Login/Register

  • MySQL Database

Phase 3

  • AI Resume Generator

Phase 4

  • AI Cover Letter Generator

Phase 5

  • Technical & HR Interview Generator

Phase 6

  • ATS Score Checker

Phase 7

  • PDF Export

Phase 8

  • Dashboard + User History

Phase 9

  • GitHub Upload + Deployment

I suggest we build this exactly like a real production application, module by module. Once you finish Phase 1 and confirm it runs on your laptop, we'll move to Phase 2 (Login + MySQL Database) with complete code. That approach will make debugging much easier and give you a polished project for your portfolio.


You said:

on which app should we start with our project

ChatGPT said:

For this project, I strongly recommend using Visual Studio Code (VS Code). It's the industry standard for Python and web development, and most recruiters and developers use it.

🛠 Software You'll Need

1. Visual Studio Code ⭐ (Main IDE)

This is where you'll write all your code.

Install these VS Code extensions:

  • Python

  • Pylance

  • HTML CSS Support

  • Live Server

  • Bootstrap 5 Snippets

  • Auto Rename Tag

  • GitHub Copilot (optional)

2. Python

Install the latest stable version (3.12 or newer).

While installing, make sure to check:

✅ Add Python to PATH

Then verify:

Bash
python --version
pip --version

3. XAMPP

We'll use XAMPP only for its MySQL server.

Start:

  • ✅ MySQL

You don't need Apache for this Flask project.

4. MySQL Workbench

We'll use this to:

  • Create the database

  • View tables

  • Run SQL queries

5. Git

Later, we'll upload the project to GitHub.

Verify:

Bash
git --version

📁 Create Your Project Folder

For example:

D:\Projects\AI-Placement-Assistant

Open VS Code:

File → Open Folder → AI-Placement-Assistant

Then open the integrated terminal:

Terminal → New Terminal

📦 Create Virtual Environment

Run:

Bash
python -m venv venv

Activate it:

Windows

Bash
venv\Scripts\activate

You should see:

(venv) D:\Projects\AI-Placement-Assistant>

📥 Install Required Libraries

Bash
pip install flask
pip install groq
pip install pymysql
pip install python-dotenv
pip install reportlab
pip install flask-login

📂 Folder Structure

AI-Placement-Assistant/
│
├── app.py
├── requirements.txt
├── config.py
│
├── templates/
│
├── static/
│   ├── css/
│   ├── js/
│   └── images/
│
└── modules/

My Recommendation for You

Since you're a beginner building this for your portfolio, use this setup:

SoftwarePurpose
VS CodeCoding
Python 3.12+Backend
FlaskWeb Framework
XAMPP (MySQL only)Database
MySQL WorkbenchDatabase GUI
GitVersion Control
GitHubPortfolio

This is exactly the kind of stack many fresher Python and Software Developer projects use.

Before we write any code, install these and tell me:

  1. ✅ VS Code installed?

  2. ✅ Python installed?

  3. ✅ XAMPP installed?

  4. ✅ MySQL Workbench installed?

  5. ✅ Git installed?

Once you confirm, we'll set up the project properly and build the homepage UI first, then connect the database, and finally integrate the AI features. 🚀


You said:

what should we select for msi insatllation

ChatGPT said:

Perfect! I can see you're on the MySQL Installer → Choosing a Setup Type screen.

For our AI Placement Assistant project, select:

✅ Full

Why?
It installs everything you may need:

  • ✅ MySQL Server

  • ✅ MySQL Workbench (GUI for databases)

  • ✅ MySQL Shell

  • ✅ Connectors (Python can connect easily)

  • ✅ Documentation and utilities

This is the easiest option for beginners and avoids missing components later.

Do NOT choose:

  • ❌ Server only (you won't get MySQL Workbench)

  • ❌ Client only (no database server)

  • ❌ Custom (unless you know exactly what you're doing)

Next Steps

  1. Select Full

  2. Click Next

  3. Click Execute when it shows the products to install.

  4. During configuration:

    • Config Type: Development Computer

    • Connectivity: Keep default port 3306

    • Authentication Method: Use the recommended default.

    • Set a root password (IMPORTANT: remember it).

I recommend using something simple for now, for example:

Root Password: omkar@123

(You can choose your own password, just don't forget it because we'll need it when connecting Python to MySQL.)

📸 When you reach the "Type and Networking" or "Accounts and Roles (root password)" screen, send me another screenshot. I'll guide you through every step so the installation is configured perfectly for our project. 🚀


You said:

whats this

ChatGPT said:

This is part of the Git installation. 😊

Git is asking:

"Which text editor should Git use when it needs to open a file for editing?"

Since we're going to use Visual Studio Code for our AI Placement Assistant project, the best choice is:

✅ Select: Use Visual Studio Code as Git's default editor

Click the dropdown and choose:

Use Visual Studio Code as Git's default editor

If you don't see it, that's okay—you can leave it as Vim and continue. Later, after installing VS Code, we can easily change it.

My recommendation:

  • If VS Code is already installed → Select Visual Studio Code.

  • If VS Code is not installed yet → Leave the default (Vim) and click Next.

It won't affect our project either way.

📌 One question: Have you already installed VS Code, or are you installing Git before VS Code?

That will determine which option is best for you.


You said:

insatlled with vs still not showing the next option what should we do or proceeedd with vim

ChatGPT said:

Yes, proceed with Vim. 👍

From your screenshot, I can see that "Next" is disabled after selecting VS Code. This sometimes happens if Git cannot properly detect the VS Code installation.

Option 1 (Recommended)

Change the dropdown back to:

Use Vim (the ubiquitous text editor) as Git's default editor

Then Next should become clickable.

Don't worry! This will not affect our project at all. In day-to-day use, you will still write all your code in VS Code.

Option 2 (Later)

After Git is installed, we can tell Git to use VS Code by running this command once:

Bash
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"

So even if you install with Vim now, you can switch to VS Code later in 10 seconds.

My advice:

✅ Select Vim
➡️ Click Next
➡️ Continue the installation with the default options.

As you go through the next Git screens, just send me screenshots if you're unsure. I'll tell you exactly which options to choose so we get your development environment ready for building the AI Placement Assistant. 🚀


You said:

what next?

ChatGPT said:

Perfect! 👍 You're doing great.

For this screen:

Adjusting the name of the initial branch

✅ Keep the default:

● Let Git decide

(Exactly as it is selected in your screenshot.)

Then click Next.

For the upcoming Git installation screens, choose these options:

1. Adjusting your PATH environment

✅ Select:
Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software
(This is usually the recommended option.)

2. Choosing HTTPS transport backend

✅ Select:
Use the OpenSSL library

3. Configuring the line ending conversions

✅ Select:
Checkout Windows-style, commit Unix-style line endings
(Recommended)

4. Configuring the terminal emulator

✅ Select:
Use MinTTY (the default terminal of MSYS2)

5. Choose the default behavior of git pull

✅ Keep the default:
Fast-forward or merge

6. Choose a credential helper

✅ Keep the default:
Git Credential Manager

7. Configuring extra options

✅ Enable:

  • ✔ Enable file system caching

❌ Leave unchecked:

  • Enable symbolic links

8. Experimental options

❌ Leave everything unchecked.

Then click:
Install → Finish

After Git is installed

Open VS Code and open a new terminal (`Ctrl + ``).

Type:

Bash
git --version
python --version

Take a screenshot of the terminal and send it to me.

Then we'll start building our AI Placement Assistant project by:

  1. Creating the project folder.

  2. Setting up the Python virtual environment.

  3. Installing Flask and the other libraries.

  4. Building the homepage UI.

We're almost ready to start coding! 🚀

ScreenSelect This
Choosing the default editor✅ Use Vim (or VS Code if it works)

ChatGPT said:

Awesome! 🎉

So far you should have:

  • ✅ VS Code installed

  • ✅ Git installed

  • ✅ Python installed (I assume)

  • ⏳ MySQL installation (we'll do this after setting up the project if not already done)

Now let's officially start building your AI Placement Assistant.

Step 1: Create the Project Folder

Create a folder anywhere you like, for example:

D:\Projects\AI-Placement-Assistant

or

C:\Users\OMKAR\Documents\AI-Placement-Assistant

(I recommend avoiding the Downloads folder.)

Step 2: Open the Folder in VS Code

  1. Open VS Code.

  2. Click File → Open Folder.

  3. Select the AI-Placement-Assistant folder.

Step 3: Open Terminal

In VS Code:

Terminal → New Terminal

You should see something like:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR\Documents\AI-Placement-Assistant>

Step 4: Verify Installations

Type these commands one by one:

Bash
git --version
Bash
python --version
Bash
pip --version

Step 5: Create Virtual Environment

Run:

Bash
python -m venv venv

Then activate it:

Windows PowerShell

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

If you are using Command Prompt:

cmd
venv\Scripts\activate

You should then see:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\Documents\AI-Placement-Assistant>

Step 6: Install Required Libraries

Run:

Bash
pip install flask groq pymysql python-dotenv reportlab flask-login

📸 Your Next Task

Open VS Code, create the project folder, open the terminal, and send me a screenshot after you run:

Bash
git --version
python --version
pip --version

Once I verify everything is working correctly, we'll create the project structure and write the very first code for our AI Placement Assistant homepage. 🚀

You said:

how to open powershell

ChatGPT said:

No problem! 😊 There are several easy ways to open PowerShell.

Method 1 (Easiest) ⭐

  1. Press Windows Key on your keyboard.

  2. Type:

PowerShell

  1. Click Windows PowerShell (or PowerShell).

A blue or black window will open.

Method 2

Press:

Windows + X

A menu will appear.

Click:

  • Windows PowerShell

  • or Terminal (on Windows 11)

Either one is fine.

Method 3 (Inside VS Code)

Since we'll be coding in VS Code:

  1. Open VS Code.

  2. Click Terminal → New Terminal.

  3. A terminal opens at the bottom.

It will usually say something like:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR>

The PS means you're already using PowerShell.

Once you have it open

Type these commands one by one:

PowerShell
where.exe python

Press Enter.

Then type:

PowerShell
where.exe git

Press Enter.

📸 Send me a screenshot or copy the output here.

That will tell us exactly why Python and Git aren't being recognized, and we'll fix it in just a couple of minutes. 🚀


You said:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR> where.exe python C:\Users\OMKAR\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\python.exe PS C:\Users\OMKAR> where.exe git D:\Git\cmd\git.exe PS C:\Users\OMKAR>

ChatGPT said:

Perfect! This tells us exactly what's happening. 👍

✅ Git is installed correctly

D:\Git\cmd\git.exe

Git is fine.

If git --version doesn't work in your current terminal, simply close PowerShell and open a new one. Then run:

PowerShell
git --version

It should work.

❌ Python PATH is the problem

You are getting:

C:\Users\OMKAR\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\python.exe

This is just the Microsoft Store shortcut, not your actual Python installation.

Let's find your real Python installation.

In PowerShell, run:

PowerShell
Get-ChildItem "C:\Users\OMKAR\AppData\Local\Programs\Python"

If that gives an error, run:

PowerShell
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files" | findstr Python

Alternatively, open File Explorer and check if one of these folders exists:

C:\Users\OMKAR\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\

or

C:\Program Files\Python313\

or

C:\Program Files\Python\

Tell me the exact folder name you see (for example, Python313).

One quick question

Can you open Python IDLE from the Start Menu?

Press the Windows key and type:

IDLE

If you see something like IDLE (Python 3.13 64-bit), click it.

If it opens, then Python is installed and we only need to fix the PATH.

If it doesn't appear, then Python needs to be installed properly.

Once you tell me:

  1. Whether IDLE opens, and

  2. What folder you find (Python313, etc.),

I'll help you fix the PATH in about 2 minutes, and then these commands will work:

PowerShell
git --version
python --version
pip --version

After that, we'll start coding the AI Placement Assistant. 🚀

You said:

ChatGPT said:

That explains the problem. 👍

If the folder contains Scripts but python.exe is missing, then your Python installation is incomplete or corrupted.

The easiest and cleanest fix is to reinstall Python.

Step 1: Download Python

Go to the official website:

https://www.python.org/downloads/

Download the latest Python 3.13.x (64-bit) installer.

Step 2: Run the Installer

VERY IMPORTANT: On the first installation screen, check this box:

☑ Add python.exe to PATH

Then click:

Install Now

(Do not choose "Customize Installation" unless necessary.)

Step 3: Verify Installation

After installation finishes, close all PowerShell windows and open a new one.

Run:

PowerShell
python --version
PowerShell
pip --version
PowerShell
where.exe python

You should see something similar to:

Python 3.13.7

pip 25.x.x from C:\Users\OMKAR\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\site-packages\pip

C:\Users\OMKAR\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\python.exe

Step 4: Verify Git

Run:

PowerShell
git --version

You should get something like:

git version 2.54.0.windows.1

One small thing before reinstalling:

Can you tell me whether you installed Python from:

  • python.org installer

  • Microsoft Store

  • or some other source?

Also, if possible, send me a screenshot of the folder that contains the Scripts folder but no python.exe. I just want to confirm we're looking at the correct directory before you reinstall.

Here's exactly what to do:

https://www.python.org/downloads/windows/

You said:

can i go with 3.14

ChatGPT said:

Yes, you can, but I actually recommend Python 3.13.x for our project. 👍

Why not 3.14 right now?

Our AI Placement Assistant will use packages like:

  • Flask

  • PyMySQL

  • Groq

  • Flask-Login

  • ReportLab

When a new Python version (like 3.14) is released, some libraries may take a little time to fully support it.

My recommendation:

🥇 Best Choice: Python 3.13.7 (64-bit)

It's stable, well-supported, and perfect for:

  • AI projects

  • Flask web development

  • MySQL

  • GitHub portfolio projects

Many companies and developers don't immediately switch to the newest Python version for this reason.

During installation, make sure you do this:

✅ Check:

☑ Add python.exe to PATH

Then click:

Install Now

After installing, open a new PowerShell and run:

PowerShell
python --version
pip --version
git --version

If all three commands work, we'll start building the AI Placement Assistant project immediately. 🚀

My advice: Go with Python 3.13.7 (64-bit) rather than 3.14 for maximum compatibility and fewer setup issues.

ChatGPT said:

Perfect, Omkar! 🎉🎉

Your development environment is now fully ready.

✅ Python 3.13.13
✅ pip 26.0.1
✅ Git 2.54.0

Now we can officially start building your AI Placement Assistant.

Step 1: Create the Project Folder

In PowerShell, run:

PowerShell
cd Desktop
mkdir AI-Placement-Assistant
cd AI-Placement-Assistant

(You can also create it in Documents if you prefer.)

Step 2: Open the Project in VS Code

Run:

PowerShell
code .

If VS Code opens, great!

If it says 'code' is not recognized, simply:

  • Open VS Code manually.

  • Click File → Open Folder.

  • Select AI-Placement-Assistant.

Step 3: Create Virtual Environment

Inside the VS Code terminal:

PowerShell
python -m venv venv

Activate it:

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

You should now see:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\Desktop\AI-Placement-Assistant>

Step 4: Install Libraries

Run:

PowerShell
pip install flask groq pymysql python-dotenv reportlab flask-login

After installation:

PowerShell
pip freeze > requirements.txt

Step 5: Create Project Structure

Inside VS Code create these files and folders:

AI-Placement-Assistant/
│
├── app.py
├── config.py
├── requirements.txt
├── .env
│
├── templates/
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── resume.html
│   ├── coverletter.html
│   ├── interview.html
│   ├── ats.html
│   └── quiz.html
│
├── static/
│   ├── css/
│   │    └── style.css
│   │
│   ├── js/
│   └── images/
│
└── modules/

Step 6: Create app.py

Paste this:

Python
from flask import Flask, render_template

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def home():
    return render_template("index.html")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

Step 7: Create templates/index.html

HTML
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>AI Placement Assistant</title>
</head>

<body>

    <h1 align="center">
        AI Placement Assistant
    </h1>

    <h3 align="center">
        Welcome Omkar 🚀
    </h3>

</body>
</html>

Step 8: Run the Project

In the terminal:

PowerShell
python app.py

You should see:

* Running on http://127.0.0.1:5000

Open your browser and visit:

http://127.0.0.1:5000

You should see:

AI Placement Assistant

Welcome Omkar 🚀

Before we continue:

Please do only these first 8 steps.

Then tell me one of these:

  • ✅ "VS Code opened."

  • ✅ "Virtual environment activated."

  • ✅ "Homepage is running."

Once you reach that point, we'll build a professional Bootstrap dashboard (much better than this basic page) and then connect it to MySQL and the Groq API.

We'll build this project exactly like a real software engineer would. 🚀

For more information, see about_Execution_Policies at https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=135170. At line:1 char:1 + .\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 + ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ + CategoryInfo : SecurityError: (:) [], PSSe curityException + FullyQualifiedErrorId : UnauthorizedAccess PS C:\Users\OMKAR>

ChatGPT said:

No worries, Omkar! 👍 This is a very common Windows PowerShell issue.

I also noticed one small thing:

You created the virtual environment in:

C:\Users\OMKAR\

instead of inside:

C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant\

Let's fix both things properly.

Step 1: Go into your project folder

Run:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant

Now your prompt should become:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

Step 2: Create the virtual environment again

Run:

PowerShell
python -m venv venv

You should now see a venv folder appear inside your project folder in VS Code.

Step 3: Allow script execution (one-time setup)

Run this command exactly:

PowerShell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned

It will ask:

Do you want to change the execution policy?
[Y] Yes  [A] Yes to All  [N] No

Type:

Y

and press Enter.

Step 4: Activate the virtual environment

Now run:

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

If everything is correct, you'll see:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

🎉 That means your project environment is ready.

One small cleanup (optional)

Since you accidentally created a venv folder in C:\Users\OMKAR, you can delete it later. For now, just ignore it and create the new one inside the project folder.

Please run these commands in order:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant

python -m venv venv

Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned

.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

Then tell me what your terminal shows. If you see (venv) at the beginning, we're ready to install Flask and start coding our AI Placement Assistant. 🚀

Let's not fight with PATH manually yet.

Step 1: Find the actual Python executable

In PowerShell, run this command:

PowerShell
py --version

If that works, then run:

PowerShell
py -3.13 -m venv venv

and tell me what happens.

If py --version also fails

Run this instead:

PowerShell
where.exe py

and copy the output here.

Quick explanation

Windows has two launchers:

  • python

  • py

Sometimes python is broken because of the Microsoft Store alias, while py works perfectly.

We already know Python itself is installed because IDLE opens. So there's a very good chance that py will work and we can continue immediately.

Just run:

PowerShell
py --version

and tell me the output. 🚀


You said:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR> py --version Python 3.13.13 PS C:\Users\OMKAR> py -3.13 -m venv venv PS C:\Users\OMKAR>

ChatGPT said:

Excellent! 🎉🎉

This confirms that your Python installation is perfectly fine. The only issue is the python command alias, so from now on we'll use py, which is the standard Windows launcher.

Now, first go back into your project folder:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant

Then create the virtual environment inside the project:

PowerShell
py -3.13 -m venv venv

After that, allow PowerShell scripts (one-time setup):

PowerShell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned

When it asks:

[Y] Yes  [A] Yes to All  [N] No

Type:

Y

and press Enter.

Now activate the virtual environment:

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

If everything is correct, your terminal should change to:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

One small recommendation

Since python is currently mapped to the Microsoft Store alias, we'll use py for all Python commands during setup:

PowerShell
py app.py
py -m pip install flask

Later, we can fix the Windows alias if you want, but it's not necessary for our project.

Your next step:

Run these commands:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant
py -3.13 -m venv venv
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

Then tell me whether you see:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

Once you see (venv), we'll install Flask and create the first version of your AI Placement Assistant. 🚀


You said:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant> py -3.13 -m venv venv PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant> Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant> [Y] Yes [A] Yes to All [N] No At line:1 char:5 + [Y] Yes [A] Yes to All [N] No + ~~~ Unexpected token 'Yes' in expression or statement. + CategoryInfo : ParserErr or: (:) [], ParentContainsErrorReco rdException + FullyQualifiedErrorId : Unexpecte dToken PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

ChatGPT said:

No problem! 😊 You didn't do anything wrong.

What happened is that PowerShell did not ask for confirmation, so when you manually typed:

[Y] Yes  [A] Yes to All  [N] No

PowerShell tried to execute it as a command.

Let's check if the policy actually changed.

Run this command:

PowerShell
Get-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser

Tell me what it prints.

If it prints:

RemoteSigned

✅ Great! Just run:

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

If it prints:

Undefined

or

Restricted

then run this command exactly:

PowerShell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned -Force

Then activate the virtual environment:

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

We're one command away from getting:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

After that, we'll install Flask and start coding your AI Placement Assistant. 🚀

Just run:

PowerShell
Get-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser

and tell me the output.


You said:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR> Get-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned PS C:\Users\OMKAR>

ChatGPT said:

Perfect! 🎉

RemoteSigned means the execution policy is already set correctly. ✅

The only reason your virtual environment didn't activate earlier is that you were back in:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR>

instead of your project folder.

Run these two commands exactly:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant

Then:

PowerShell
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

If everything is correct, you'll see:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

📌 Important: Don't create another venv. You already created it successfully.

If you get an error saying:

Cannot find path '...\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1'

then the virtual environment was accidentally created outside the project folder. In that case, simply run:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant
py -3.13 -m venv venv
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

Just run:

PowerShell
cd C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant
.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

and tell me exactly what your terminal shows. Once we see (venv), we'll install Flask and start building your AI Placement Assistant. 🚀

At the bottom of VS Code, does it show something like:

PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

If activation is successful, the terminal will change to:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

If you get an error, don't worry.

Just tell me exactly what your terminal prompt currently shows (for example, PS C:\Users\OMKAR> or PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>), and whether running the activation command gives an error.

Also, one quick question:

or do you also see files like app.py, templates, etc.?

ChatGPT said:

YES!! 🎉🎉🎉

Congratulations, Omkar! Your Python virtual environment is now working perfectly.

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

This means:

  • ✅ Python installed

  • ✅ Git installed

  • ✅ VS Code configured

  • ✅ Virtual Environment created

  • ✅ PowerShell fixed

Now we can finally start coding! 🚀

Step 1: Install Required Libraries

Copy and paste this command:

PowerShell
pip install flask groq pymysql python-dotenv reportlab flask-login

Wait until it finishes (it may take 1-2 minutes).

Step 2: Save the Dependencies

Run:

PowerShell
pip freeze > requirements.txt

You should now see a new file called:

requirements.txt

on the left side.

Step 3: Create Our First File

In the left sidebar:

Right Click on AI-Placement-Assistant
→ New File
→ Type:

app.py

Press Enter.

Step 4: Add This Code

Paste this into app.py:

Python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def home():
    return "<h1>Welcome to AI Placement Assistant 🚀</h1>"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

Save the file (Ctrl + S).

Step 5: Run Your First Flask Application

In the terminal, type:

PowerShell
py app.py

(or you can use:)

PowerShell
python app.py

since the venv is active.

You should see something similar to:

* Running on http://127.0.0.1:5000

Hold Ctrl and click on:

http://127.0.0.1:5000

Your browser should open and display:

Welcome to AI Placement Assistant 🚀

Small milestone achieved 🏆

You've successfully completed the hardest part:

  • Environment Setup ✅

  • Virtual Environment ✅

  • Flask Installation (next) ⏳

  • First Web App ⏳

Now, before we build the professional dashboard, complete Steps 1 to 5 above and tell me one thing:

"Flask app is running."

Then we'll create the complete project structure (templates, static, modules, CSS, Bootstrap UI) and start building your portfolio-grade AI Placement Assistant. 🚀

ChatGPT said:

Perfect! 🎉

That message is not an error. It simply means a newer version of pip is available.

You can safely ignore it for now. We don't need to update pip before starting the project.

Your terminal:

(venv) PS C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant>

means everything is working correctly. ✅

Next Step: Create your first Python file

1. On the left sidebar:

  • Right-click AI-Placement-Assistant

  • Click New File

  • Name it:

app.py

2. Open app.py and paste this code:

Python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def home():
    return "<h1>Welcome to AI Placement Assistant 🚀</h1>"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

Save it with Ctrl + S.

Run your first Flask app

In the terminal, type:

PowerShell
py app.py

You should see something similar to:

* Serving Flask app 'app'
* Debug mode: on
* Running on http://127.0.0.1:5000

Then:

  • Hold Ctrl

  • Click on http://127.0.0.1:5000

Your browser should open and display:

Welcome to AI Placement Assistant 🚀

This will be the first working version of the project that you'll later upload to GitHub and add to your resume.

After you run py app.py, tell me exactly what appears in the terminal. 🚀

ChatGPT said:

That's completely normal! 👍

It usually means you've right-clicked on the Explorer background or the folder is collapsed.

Try this:

  1. Look at the left sidebar.

  2. You should see something like:

AI-PLACEMENT-ASSISTANT
│
└── venv

  1. Click once on the "AI-PLACEMENT-ASSISTANT" folder name to highlight it.

  2. Now look at the top of the Explorer panel (above the folder list). You should see icons like:

📄 New File | 📁 New Folder

Click the 📄 New File icon.

If you still don't see it:

You can also use the keyboard shortcut:

  • Click on the AI-Placement-Assistant folder.

  • Press:

Ctrl + N

(No, that usually opens a new window, so better use the Explorer icon method.)

Or simply:

  1. Click the New File (📄) icon at the top.

  2. Type:

app.py

  1. Press Enter.

Another easy way:

In VS Code, click:

File → New Text File

Then immediately press Ctrl + S.

Navigate to:

C:\Users\OMKAR\AI-Placement-Assistant

Save the file as:

app.py

Can you tell me what you see on the left sidebar?

Something like:

AI-PLACEMENT-ASSISTANT
> venv

or

AI-PLACEMENT-ASSISTANT
▼ venv

Also, do you see a small 📄 (New File) icon at the top of the Explorer panel?
